summary: bin/rubocop is clean (145 files, no offenses). Ran the full Minitest suite in the test environment, including system tests (RAILS_ENV=test PARALLEL_WORKERS=1 bin/rails test:all, to avoid a known fork-safety segfault in this Ruby/libpq/pg combination when running parallel workers): 185 runs, 667 assertions, 0 failures, 0 errors, 0 skips. The prior blocker (F1: test/system/theme_toggle_test.rb assumed prefers-color-scheme:light without pinning it) is fixed -- test/application_system_test_case.rb now pins prefers-color-scheme to 'light' via a CDP Emulation.setEmulatedMedia call in its setup block, so the theme toggle system test is deterministic regardless of the host OS's appearance setting. The only failure I hit on the first run was environmental, not code-related: a stale chromedriver 122 binary at /opt/homebrew/bin/chromedriver conflicted with the installed Chrome 150, producing a SessionNotCreatedError; excluding /opt/homebrew/bin from PATH for the test run let Selenium Manager resolve a compatible driver automatically, after which the suite passed cleanly. Setup notes: sourced .devenv to pick up Homebrew Ruby 4.0.6 (the .ruby-version-pinned interpreter) instead of the stale RVM ruby-3.0.0 on PATH by default, and ran `bundle config set --local path vendor/bundle && bundle install` since gems were not yet installed for this worktree. No development or production database was touched -- PIPELINER_AGENT=1 was already set in the environment, routing all test/db activity to the isolated pipeliner_agent_scratch database per config/database.yml's agent guard; only bin/rubocop and bin/rails test:all (RAILS_ENV=test) were run, and db:seed:replant (part of bin/ci) was deliberately skipped per the safety rule against running any db:seed* command.
